title: "Customer Churn Prediction"
subtitle: "Stage 2 of 3 — Data-Driven Engineering Portfolio"
author: "Shikhar Sinha | Production & Industrial Engineering, IIT Delhi"
date: "July 1, 2026"
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: true
    code-fold: true
    embed-resources: true
---

## Executive Summary
> Trained and compared three classification models (Logistic Regression, Random Forest, XGBoost) on 7,043 customer records to predict churn. Logistic Regression achieved the highest cross-validated ROC-AUC at 0.8463, while the balanced XGBoost model optimized for active attrition retrieval achieved a test ROC-AUC of 0.8364 and caught 75.7% of churners. SHAP analysis identified a poor value perception (`charge_per_service`) and short customer lifecycles (`tenure`) as the dominant churn predictors. Risk segmentation identified 1,662 customers in the Critical Risk tier, representing an estimated $116,600 net retention opportunity.

## Problem Statement
Acquiring a new customer costs 5–7× more than retaining an existing one. Identifying at-risk customers before they leave is the highest-ROI intervention in customer management. By leveraging predictive machine learning models, the company can transition from defensive, reactive damage control to automated, high-precision customer preservation.

## Dataset
| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Source | IBM Telco Customer Churn (Kaggle) |
| Rows | 7,043 customers |
| Features | 21 raw → 27 after preprocessing and feature engineering |
| Churn rate | 26.5% |
| Train/test | 80/20 stratified split (5,634 Train | 1,409 Test rows) |

## Key Findings
1. **Contract Structure:** Month-to-month contracts churn at a staggering **42.7%** compared to just **2.8%** for long-term 2-year agreements.
2. **The Attrition Window:** Attrition is heavily front-loaded. The median tenure of churned customers is just **10 months** compared to **38 months** for retained clients, with a massive drop-off spike occurring in months 1–3.
3. **Model Performance:** * Logistic Regression Cross-Validated ROC-AUC: **0.8463**
   * Random Forest Cross-Validated ROC-AUC: **0.8412**
   * XGBoost Test Set ROC-AUC: **0.8364** | Test Set PR-AUC: **0.6467**
4. **Top SHAP Driver:** **`charge_per_service`** — High values (paying a premium price for very few bundled ecosystem services) dramatically increase individual customer churn risk.


# Recommendations
1. Contract upgrade incentive for M2M customers in months 1–12
2. Proactive onboarding for new customers (tenure < 6 months)
3. Bundle promotion for customers with ≤ 2 services
